title: common/hugo: Include aom, dav1d, and libavif in commands/env output
author: jmooring
body:
Currently, the `hugo env` command prints:

```text
hugo v0.164.0-ce2470e7012b5ab5fc4e10ebe4027e9f8d9e00dc+withdeploy linux/amd64 BuildDate=2026-07-06T16:39:30Z VendorInfo=gohugoio
GOOS="linux"
GOARCH="amd64"
GOVERSION="go1.26.3"
github.com/webmproject/libwebp="v1.6.0"
github.com/sass/dart-sass/protocol="3.2.0"
github.com/sass/dart-sass/compiler="1.101.0"
github.com/sass/dart-sass/implementation="1.101.0"
```

I think it would make sense to also list...

```text
aomedia.googlesource.com/aom="v3.14.1"
code.videolan.org/videolan/dav1d="1.5.3"
github.com/AOMediaCodec/libavif="v.14.1"
```

...assuming I have the correct source strings.

See:

<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/a198116669fdfba2eeda4f1d63c055001a10142f/common/hugo/hugo.go#L213-L214>.

title: snap: User's global Git core.quotepath setting not read
author: jmooring
body:
This means that `GitInfo` is not available for a content file named `hugö.md`. 

Related to https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/issues/9810.

---

And that doesn't make sense given:

https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/a198116669fdfba2eeda4f1d63c055001a10142f/snap/snapcraft.yaml#L63-L68

And:

title: Add css.ChromaStyles
author: bep
body:
We have a [CLI command](https://gohugo.io/commands/hugo_gen_chromastyles/#hugo-gen-chromastyles) for this, with Chroma's recent addition of dark/light modes, it would be convenient to generate these on-the-fly. This would allow this to be configured in `hugo.toml` and also allow the user an easier way to find the correct settings.

E.g.

```handlebars
{{ $dark := css.ChromaStyles dict 
  "targetPath" "css/croma_dark.css"
   "style" "monokai"
   "modeSelector" "dark"
    "mode" "dark"
}}
<link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ $dark.RelPermalink }}">
```

This would be even more useful if we fix #15103.


* The options would be `targetPath` + the options in https://gohugo.io/commands/hugo_gen_chromastyles/#hugo-gen-chromastyles
* Creating the `Resource` could be delegated to `create.Client.FromString`.

title: Add an allowlist for intentionally unexecuted templates when using `printUnusedTemplates`
author: Xeonzilla
body:
Hugo's `printUnusedTemplates` option is very useful for keeping a site's template tree clean. I would like to request a small companion mechanism to mark some templates as intentionally allowed to remain unexecuted during successful builds.

### Current behavior

`printUnusedTemplates` reports templates whose execution count is zero during the build. This is a good default behavior and I do not think it should change.

However, some templates are intentionally only used on error paths or other rare conditional paths. For example, a site may have a shared partial used to fail fast with consistent diagnostics:

```go-html-template
{{ partial "fail.html" (dict
  "message" "missing required parameter"
  "location" "..."
  "fix" "..."
) }}
```

During a successful production build, this partial is not executed, so `printUnusedTemplates` reports it as unused even though it is intentionally part of the template system.

A site can work around this by calling the partial once with a no-op argument, but that feels like a workaround: it changes template execution only to satisfy the diagnostic check.

## Proposal

Add a config option to exclude specific templates from `printUnusedTemplates` warnings, for example:

```toml
ignoreUnusedTemplates = [
  "_partials/fail.html",
]
```

Glob patterns would also be useful, but exact template names alone would already solve the core problem:

```toml
ignoreUnusedTemplates = [
  "_partials/fail.html",
  "_partials/debug/*.html",
]
```

The important part is that this would not change the meaning of `printUnusedTemplates`: templates would still be considered "used" only when actually executed. The option would only suppress warnings for templates the project explicitly declares as intentionally retained.

## Use cases

This would help with templates that are valid and intentional but may not execute in a successful build:

- shared error/fail-fast partials
- debug-only partials
- optional theme extension points
- templates used only by certain content states, languages, output formats, or build environments
- rare shortcode/render-hook variants that a theme wants to keep available

## Alternatives considered

Changing printUnusedTemplates to count static references as usage would make the warning less precise, and could hide templates that are referenced but never actually reached.

A template-local marker such as a comment directive could also work, but a config allowlist seems simpler and keeps the diagnostic policy in site configuration.

title: tests: don't depend on specific esbuild version for tests
author: kapouer
body:
Hi,
https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/commit/884439b9a21dfbc4ac28e8534ef3764d04db0ab3

shows that even esbuild patch releases can change the outcome of the file / directory names,
`en/mybatch/chunk-TOZKWCDE.js` becoming `en/mybatch/chunk-MO3Q4WT6.js`.

The tests shouldn't be pinned to such a specific esbuild behavior.

In particular, we see this in Debian, where an esbuild update makes hugo test suite fail.

Still, it's not a catastrophic nor urgent problem (in Debian we can just remove the assertions and be done with it).

title: Feature Request: Introduce hugo fix to automate breaking change migrations (e.g., getJSON / getCache removal)
author: hitzhangjie
body:
Hi Hugo Team,

I have been happily running Hugo v0.111+extended for several years with a forked dokz theme, and it has worked flawlessly.

Recently, I migrated to a new machine and installed the latest v0.164+extended. Upon running hugo server, I encountered multiple breaking changes related to the deprecation and removal of getCache, getJSON, and other functions.

**While I am willing to spend time upgrading my site, the current workflow feels incredibly disruptive. To get the site running again, I have to:**

- Update the main configuration files.

- Dig into the layout files (often deeply embedded in themes) to replace old functions with their modern alternatives (like resources.GetRemote).

- Update my GitHub Actions workflows.

This is highly time-consuming. Therefore, I would love to propose a feature: hugo fix.

**Here is why I think this is necessary:**

- Automated Alternatives: When a feature is deprecated or removed, Hugo almost always introduces a standard alternative. A command like hugo fix could automatically scan and migrate the project's codebase to the new syntax.

- The "Theme User" Dilemma: A significant portion of Hugo users are not theme developers; they choose a well-designed theme and customize it. When breaking changes break the theme's layouts, average users are forced to debug Go templates, which they might not be familiar with. This is especially painful if the upstream theme is no longer actively maintained.

I sincerely suggest that the Hugo team considers introducing a migration tool like hugo fix to improve the developer experience and make major version upgrades much smoother.

Thank you for your amazing work on Hugo!

title: Add importContext to resources.Copy
author: bep

title: hugo --gc deletes in-use image cache files when on-disk path case differs from the cache key (case-insensitive filesystems)
author: cybernagl
body: ### What happens On a case-insensitive filesystem (macOS APFS, Windows), `hugo --gc` deletes image cache files under `resources/_gen` that the build **actively uses**, if the on-disk path case differs from the cache key. The build itself works fine (the case-insensitive filesystem resolves the read), but the pruner's usage check is a case-sensitive string comparison, so the just-used file is considered unused and removed. The relevant code is in `cache/filecache/filecache_pruner.go` (unchanged on master): ```go if !shouldRemove && c.entryLocker.seen.Len() > 0 { // Remove it if it's not been touched/used in the last build. shouldRemove = !c.entryLocker.seen.Has(name) } ``` `seen` contains the cache key (for images: the lowercased target path, via `ImageCache.getOrCreate` → `c.fcache.ReadOrCreate(relTargetPath, …)`), while `name` is the real on-disk name from the filesystem walk. If they differ only in case, `seen.Has(name)` is false and the file is deleted. ### Why the case can differ in practice Page paths have been lowercased since the v0.123 page-system rewrite, so cache keys for page-bundle images are lowercase. Caches created by older Hugo versions preserve the source directory case (e.g. `resources/_gen/images/collections/PastFuture/…`). Such caches are common in the wild: committed to git for CI cold-start speed, restored by CI cache plugins, or simply carried forward on a developer machine across Hugo upgrades. The result is a permanent rebuild/evict cycle: `--gc` deletes the in-use entries → the next build silently re-processes all of them → the new files land inside the surviving mixed-case directory (the prune walk visits directories before deleting their files, so the directory is never seen empty and survives; on a case-insensitive filesystem the re-created file then lands in it) → the next `--gc` deletes them again. On the real site where we found this, every build re-scaled 267 images (~35 s per build, forever). ### Minimal reproduction (macOS) ```sh hugo new site repro && cd repro mkdir -p content/MyBundle layouts/_default printf 'baseURL = "https://example.org/"\ndisableKinds = ["taxonomy", "term", "rss", "sitemap"]\n' > hugo.toml printf -- '---\ntitle: My Bundle\n---\n' > content/MyBundle/index.md cp /path/to/any.jpeg content/MyBundle/cover.jpeg printf '{{ with .Resources.GetMatch "cover.jpeg" }}{{ with .Resize "100x" }}{{ end }}{{ end }}' > layouts/_default/single.html printf 'home' > layouts/_default/home.html hugo # creates resources/_gen/images/mybundle/cover_hu_.jpeg # simulate a cache written by an older Hugo (or copied from elsewhere): mv resources/_gen/images/mybundle resources/_gen/images/tmp mv resources/_gen/images/tmp resources/_gen/images/MyBundle hugo --gc # build succeeds (16 ms, cache hit), but reports "Cleaned 1" find resources -type f # -> empty: the file the build just used is gone hugo # re-processes the image (474 ms) — and writes it back # into the surviving MyBundle/ dir, so the cycle repeats ``` Observed output of the `--gc` build: ``` Processed images │ 1 Cleaned │ 1 Total in 16 ms ``` A cache file that was read 16 ms earlier is deleted as "not been touched/used in the last build". ### What I expected `--gc` should never delete a cache entry that the same build read. On case-insensitive filesystems the usage check should match the way the filesystem resolved the read — e.g. compare case-insensitively (or normalize walk names against the `seen` set) when the cache filesystem is case-insensitive. Renaming the on-disk entry to the key's case would even self-heal old caches. ### Version ``` hugo v0.164.0+extended+withdeploy darwin/arm64 BuildDate=2026-07-06T16:39:30Z VendorInfo=Homebrew ``` The pruner code is identical on current master.

title: Add a "feature switcher" for performance
author: bep
body:
I'm not sure about the naming here. This relates to #15085. That PR fixes a performance issue with that particular feature (triggered by templates.Defer or resources.PostProcess). templates.Defer or resources.PostProcess is only used by a small fraction of all Hugo sites (I'm guessing a little), yet everyone pays the price of having to scan the render writer for special tokens.

I can probably come up with more examples, but i think it would be relatively simple and worth while to have some atomic feature switchers that could be toggled. In the above example:

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Defer(ctx context.Context, args ...any) (bool, error) {
 ns.deps.Features.Defer.Enable()
}
```

Or something like that. And then do the same thing in `resources.PostProcess`.

Then the `HasBytesWriter` that is used could just pause the scanning until that particular feature is enabled.

title: Deprecate resources.PostProcess in favour of templates.Defer
author: bep
body:
* templates.Defer is a much better construct without the many limitations of resources.PostProcess.
* Theres some relatively complex code behind resources.PostProcess that I would prefer not having to maintain.

title: New content command thinks there's a path conflict for a file that doesn't exist yet
author: KunFuCutsMan
body:
I run my personal blog using Hugo for about two years ago, and it has been great!, but there have been issues with how I create pages. I have section called `posts/drafts/` where I create drafts for posts before I move them to a `posts/YEAR/MONTH` directory where I can post them. The bug happens when I create the page for a post, like this: 
hugo new content posts/drafts/trip-to-puebla/index.md
Running this command with the debug flag returns the following: 
INFO  build:  step process substep collect files 138 files_total 138 pagesources_total 71 resourcesources_total 67 duration 37.1674ms
INFO  build:  step process duration 38.2639ms
INFO  build:  step assemble duration 2.064063s
INFO  build:  step render pages 0 content 0 duration 548.6µs
INFO  build:  step render deferred count 0 duration 0s
INFO  build:  step postProcess duration 0s
INFO  build:  duration 2.1061069s
ERROR no page found for "C:\\...\\Documents\\projects\\kfcmanwebsite2\\content\\posts\\drafts\\trip-to-puebla\\index.md"; the target path conflicts with existing conten
What's funny is that a page _is_ created, but it's a placeholder page that looks like this: ``` --- title: "Content Placeholder" build: render: never list: never publishResources: false --- ``` After consulting with ChatGPT (yes you can stone me now) and testing some things here's another stuff that cursed AI and I found: * The name of the page still causes some issues, doesn't matter if its `trip-to-puebla/index.md` or `trip-to-puebla`. * Renaming the `drafts` directory and rerunning the command with the new name still throws the error * This issue **does not happen** if I run it when creating something like `hugo new content posts/2026/07/foo/index.md` One final thing, I have a branch bundle on `posts/drafts/_index.md` that has the following front matter: ```yaml title: Drafts build: render: never cascade: draft: true build: render: link weight: 9999 draft: true ``` This is so any of the pages inside `posts/drafts` don't get published. ### What version of Hugo are you using (`hugo version`)? 
hugo v0.163.3-4d22555aebf458d5d150500c9ac4bee5b24cf0d3+extended windows/amd64 BuildDate=2026-06-18T16:18:24Z VendorInfo=gohugoio
### Does this issue reproduce with the latest release? I'm using the latest version, so it _should_ be reproducible. I think. [The repo for my hugo project can be found here](https://github.com/KunFuCutsMan/kfcmanwebsite2), though it may be a little heavy.

title: Make .Render take additional data
author: bep
body:
See https://gohugo.io/methods/page/render/#article
See #15076

> PAGE.Render NAME

This has been discussed before. 

* The contract has always been that the templates that `.Renders` gets the data context as e.g. `page.html`, a `page.Page`.
* We can only pass 1 argument to Go's template renderer, which I guess is the blocking reason for not having done this sooner.

So, if we want to do:

> PAGE.Render [DATA] NAME

I'm guessing we have 2 top-level options:

* We say that if `DATA` is set, we use that as the context passed to the templates,. else `PAGE`.
* We somehow (e.g. by embeding it in a `{ DataAdditionalContext, Page }` struct or  putting it into the current `context.Context`) wrap the `Page` so you can do both `.RelPermalink` and `.DataAdditionalContext` (naming of the latter is just picked out of a hat without thinking too hard.

title: Make partial templates context-aware
author: stavnstrup
body:
I am currently working on a site, utilizing Docsy v. 0.15.0. In Docsy, all sections pages below the _docs/_ folder are rendered using _layout/docs/list.html_ which call the partial _layout/\_partials/section-index.html_. On my site, I have created folder called _references_ and placed inside the _docs_ folder, and for that folder I would like to keep using the code in the _list.html_ template, but replace the content of the _section-index.html_ partial. The documentation at https://gohugo.io/templates/new-templatesystem-overview/ states that "_We have consolidated the template lookup so it works the same across all template types_", which to me also means partials. When I create a replacement partial at _layout/\_partials/docs/references/section-index.html_, it does not work, but if I move the partial to _layout/_partials/section-index.html_, it does work. However, that will prevent me to override the behaviour in a sub-directory of the _references_ directory. ### What version of Hugo are you using (`hugo version`)? 
$ hugo version

0.163.3
### Does this issue reproduce with the latest release? Yes

title: Add  crypto.Hash(algo string)
author: bep
body:
* The func `crypto.Hash` should match the `.Data.Integrity` on fingerprinted resources with the same set of crypto algos.
* If algo not set, default to `sha256`.
* So we could to `{{ "hello world" | crypto.Hash }}` and  `{{ "hello world" | crypto.Hash "sha384" }}`

title: Add  encoding.HexDecode/Encode
author: bep
body:
See https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/issues/15060#issuecomment-4790405347

It's clumsy that you need to create a resource (making up an unique filename) to get a SRI hash.

I suggest we add `crypto.Fingerprint` with a similar signature to https://gohugo.io/functions/resources/fingerprint/

>hash.Fingerprint [ALGORITHM] any -> string

The function name is a little clumsy, but we need to keep the two very similar functions in line.

Where `any` typically is a `string` (but can also be a `Resource` -- we have similar unwrappers elsewhere).

It would then return e.g. `sha256-Yuh...rfE=`.

Note to implementer: There's some obvious code reuse potential here.

title: Aliases with .htm extension are published to the wrong path
author: ptvoinfo
body: The latest hugo version hugo v0.163.3-4d22555aebf458d5d150500c9ac4bee5b24cf0d3+extended windows/amd64 BuildDate=2026-06-18T16:18:24Z VendorInfo=gohugoio uglyUrls = true ``` content/ ├── tutorials/ │ ├── fil1.md │ └── file2.md └── tutorials.md ``` The frontmattter in tutorials.md contains 2 aliases. Hugo does not create both alias files. It worked in 0.153.2 ``` +++ title = "#tutorials_page_title" description = "#tutorials_page_desc" keywords = "#tutorials_page_kwd" layout = "single" url = "/tutorials.htm" aliases = ["/tutorials/index.html", "/tutorials/index.htm"] +++ ```

title: Implement Pandoc bibliography support
author: jmooring

title: Centralized scanning of inline script/style hashes for Content-Security-Policy
author: oschmid
body:
Inline scripts/styles are useful for performance and features like light/dark theme switching. But manually hashing these scripts/styles and updating a website's Content-Security-Policy header configuration when they change is tedious and error-prone. Getting it wrong can break site functionality or styling in production even when it works locally.

A more standard Hugo way of tracking these hashes might be to extract inline scripts/styles into separate asset files and use `resources.Get` and `resources.Fingerprint` functions to inject the contents into an HTML file and generate the hashes. But a developer has to remember this pattern every time they write an inline script/style, and most imported themes do not follow it.

As an alternative I wrote [csp_inline_hashes.go](https://github.com/oschmid/website/blob/c23b41754a264d5974b16b8b344a75019a26e406/bin/csp_inline_hashes.go) to run after `hugo build`. It scans `public/` for inline scripts/styles and writes unique sha256 hashes into a header configuration file using a text/template (with variables such as `{{ .ScriptSrcElemHashes }}`) before deployment. I think this pattern is generic enough to be useful to others that use Hugo, inline scripts/styles, and Content-Security-Policy.

Would this be useful for inclusion into Hugo itself? If so I would be willing to rewrite it as a Hugo feature and be open to discussing what configuration params it should have (e.g. different hash functions, what directory to scan, template location, output file, etc.)

A first draft of configuration params:
```
contentSecurityPolicyInlineHashes:
  enableScriptSrcElemHashes: true/false
  enableStyleSrcAttrHashes: true/false
  enableStyleSrcElemHashes: true/false
  scriptSrcElemRegex: <regex override>
  styleSrcAttrRegex: <regex override>
  styleSrcElemRegex: <regex override>
  hashAlgorithm: sha256/sha384/sha512
  # scan dir could re-use publishDir
  headersTemplate: <path to layouts/_headers or similar template file>
  outputFile: <path within publishDir to write to>
```

title: Make template name lookup case-insensitive for layout front matter and .Render
author: jmooring
body:
When looking up a template, the name supplied via the `layout` front matter parameter or as an argument to the `Page.Render` method is matched against file names using a case-sensitive comparison. Shortcode and partial template lookups are already case-insensitive. These should behave the same way.
integration test ```go // See issue 15057. func TestCaseInsensitiveLayoutLookup(t *testing.T) { t.Parallel() files := ` -- hugo.toml -- disableKinds = ['home','rss','section','sitemap','taxonomy','term'] -- content/s1/p1.md -- --- title: p1 --- -- content/s2/p2.md -- --- title: p2 layout: ab --- -- content/s2/p3.md -- --- title: p3 layout: aB --- -- content/s2/p4.md -- --- title: p4 layout: AB --- -- content/s3/p5.md -- --- title: p5 layout: cd --- -- content/s3/p6.md -- --- title: p6 layout: cD --- -- content/s3/p7.md -- --- title: p7 layout: CD --- -- content/s4/p8.md -- --- title: p8 layout: ef --- -- content/s4/p9.md -- --- title: p9 layout: eF --- -- content/s4/p10.md -- --- title: p10 layout: EF --- -- layouts/s1/p1/page.html -- {{ .Render "ab" }}|{{ .Render "aB" }}|{{ .Render "AB" }} {{ .Render "cd" }}|{{ .Render "cD" }}|{{ .Render "CD" }} {{ .Render "ef" }}|{{ .Render "eF" }}|{{ .Render "EF" }} -- layouts/s1/p1/ab.html -- ab{{- /**/ -}} -- layouts/s1/p1/cD.html -- cD{{- /**/ -}} -- layouts/s1/p1/EF.html -- EF{{- /**/ -}} -- layouts/s2/ab.html -- ab -- layouts/s3/cD.html -- cD -- layouts/s4/EF.html -- EF ` b := Test(t, files) b.AssertFileContent("public/s1/p1/index.html", "ab|ab|ab", "cD|cD|cD", "EF|EF|EF", ) b.AssertFileContent("public/s2/p2/index.html", "ab") b.AssertFileContent("public/s2/p3/index.html", "ab") b.AssertFileContent("public/s2/p4/index.html", "ab") b.AssertFileContent("public/s3/p5/index.html", "cD") b.AssertFileContent("public/s3/p6/index.html", "cD") b.AssertFileContent("public/s3/p7/index.html", "cD") b.AssertFileContent("public/s4/p8/index.html", "EF") b.AssertFileContent("public/s4/p9/index.html", "EF") b.AssertFileContent("public/s4/p10/index.html", "EF") } ```

Reference:

title: Support subdirectory paths in .Render
author: jmooring
body:
Allow `.Render "subdir/myview"` to look up a content view template named `myview` starting from `layouts/{page-path}/subdir/`, walking up the tree as usual. Note that `subdir` is not part of the tree.

This construct worked in earlier versions; we removed it in v0.146.0 with implementation of the new template system.

The purpose of such a construct is to organize content views in a dedicated directory.

Reference: <https://discourse.gohugo.io/t/silent-error-with-render-function-in-v0-163-2-extended-withdeploy/57290/9>
